description: A 'kanuro' is a choice given settled or actionable status by a person, group, or institution. The decision may be provisional or contested and still guide what happens next. 'kanu' (choose) names selection itself; 'kanuro' is the resulting matter others can record, review, or implement.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the sound through the nose, and hold stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before final true-mid rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: 'kanu' (choose) carries the opening from a velar release into deep stressed 'nu'. Final 'ro' adds a turn of the tongue and a rounded close. The settled choice acquires one further audible shape.
usage_notes: Individual decisions count. The maker and procedure come before 'kanuro' when they belong inside the noun phrase; scope and conditions follow the same rule. A separate clause may carry any relation that needs more room. Implementation and review remain later acts. Exact judgments, orders, resolutions, or other source categories retain the names and force used by their institutions.

examples.phi: ha kowela ha kanuro to phoa.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A recorded decision lets shared work proceed while its conditions and review path remain recoverable.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating decision from agreement and consent preserves dissent after a group begins to act.

semantic_domains.cognition: a choice treated as settled enough to guide later action
semantic_domains.community: a decision made within a group or institutional process
